raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I used my Barclays credit card to purchase items totaling {$430.00} from a merchant ( PayPhone ). The items ( toys, blocks, and a baby chair ) were never shipped. The merchant admitted this in writing through XXXX messages, which I provided to Barclays as part of my supporting documents. 

I opened a dispute with Barclays. Barclays issued a provisional credit on XX/XX/XXXX, but later closed the case on XX/XX/XXXX, stating that the merchant validated the transaction and that I had not followed the cancellation/refund policy. This is incorrect the issue is not about cancellation, it is about non-receipt of goods, which is a protected dispute category under the Fair Credit Billing Act.

I believe Barclays mishandled this dispute by ( 1 ) misclassifying it, and ( 2 ) refusing to review the case after I submitted proof from the merchants direct communication admitting the goods were never shipped.
